results.title: Adjournment of Debate
results.summary: Mr Sharael Taha moved to adjourn the debate on the Adjournment of Debate. The Speaker granted consent for the debate to conclude immediately. The session will resume tomorrow. No further policy measures, timelines, or operational details were discussed during this brief procedural exchange.

results.title: Additional Support to Strengthen Well-being of National Athletes
results.summary: The Government integrated the Singapore Sport Institute and National Youth Sports Institute into the High Performance Sport Institute (HPSI) in April to provide holistic support, including sport psychology, proactive mental health monitoring, and resilience training. To address financial stress, monthly Central Provident Fund top-ups were introduced for spexScholars in April, while the spexEducation Undergraduate Scholarship launched in 2024 supports academic aspirations alongside sporting ambitions. A memorandum of understanding signed in April between the Singapore Sports School and six local autonomous universities aims to enhance athlete-friendly campus practices. Additionally, the spexBusiness scheme offers employment opportunities, career development support through internships, and flexible work arrangements to help athletes balance work and sport. The Government encourages public contributions to the One Team Singapore Fund and corporate participation in the spexBusiness network to further strengthen athlete support systems.

results.title: Anxiety-causing Factors Amongst Singaporeans on Job Security and Plans to Strengthen Support for Mid-career Workers
results.summary: The Minister for Manpower directed Members to refer to the reply to Question No 41 in the Order Paper dated 23 September 2025 for details on measures safeguarding job stability and supporting mid-career workers. The transcript does not specify the factors explaining persistent anxiety regarding job security or outline the Government's concrete plans to strengthen support for workers vulnerable to restructuring. No further details on operational constraints, timelines, or eligibility conditions were provided in this exchange.

results.title: Amendments to Building Maintenance and Strata Management Act for Better Maintenance and Planning towards Ageing Buildings
results.summary: The Ministry of National Development and Building and Construction Authority will consider stakeholder suggestions regarding amendments to the Building Maintenance and Strata Management Act, including potential minimum sinking fund contributions or mandatory technical audits for older buildings, with further details shared upon review completion. BCA is conducting public education through Strata Management Guides, free online self-learning courses for council members, and Strata Insights, a quarterly publication series addressing issues like lift modernisation and long-term financial planning to help property owners manage their developments effectively.

results.title: Assessing A*STAR’s Industrial Value-add to Local Enterprises
results.summary: The Ministry assesses A*STAR's industrial value-add through three primary indicators: collaborative projects, talent secondment, and spin-offs. Over the past five years, A*STAR executed more than 2,400 projects with 925 local enterprises, providing access to shared facilities like the National Semiconductor Translation and Innovation Centre. Through the Technology for Enterprise Capability Upgrading (T-Up) programme, over 300 research scientists and engineers were seconded to 177 enterprises, with evaluation studies showing participants achieved 44% higher revenue and 22% higher employment within three years. Additionally, A*STAR-originated technologies have spun off more than 70 new companies and licensed 302 technologies to 230 local SMEs and startups, including international successes like Mirxes and MetaOptics. The Ministry aims to establish more shared facilities to further support local enterprise growth.

results.title: Assessing G20 Nations' Gold Reserves Increase and Implications for Singapore's Reserve Management Strategy
results.summary: The Minister stated that Singapore's gold holdings are sized to ensure the Reserves portfolio remains resilient and well-diversified across economic and market conditions. Global demand for gold reflects its role as a safe-haven asset during periods of economic and geopolitical uncertainty. The Minister did not specify the exact percentage of total reserves held by G20 nations, nor did she provide a defined timeline or specific factors that would trigger a reconsideration of the current gold allocation strategy. No formal review evaluating implications for the reserve management strategy was explicitly mentioned in the response.

results.title: Average Peak-hour Waiting Time at Sengkang, Buangkok and Hougang MRT Stations for City-Bound Commuters from 2021 to 2025
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport confirmed that the average peak-hour waiting times for city-bound commuters at Sengkang, Buangkok, and Hougang MRT stations were addressed in a written reply issued for Parliamentary Questions 30 and 31. This response was provided for the oral answer during the 23 September 2025 Parliament sitting. The specific numerical data for waiting times across the 2021 to 2025 period was not included in the transcript provided.

results.title: Brain-based Pedagogies and Cognitive Psychology for Preschools and Primary Schools
results.summary: The Minister confirmed that current pedagogies are already informed by neuroscience, psychology, and educational research, emphasizing hands-on learning over specific neuroscience-based assessments for preschoolers. He stated that matching children to pedagogies solely via neuroscience is inappropriate, as effective learning depends on socio-emotional and cultural factors. Regarding AI, the Ministry is leveraging existing features in the Singapore Student Learning Space to customise mathematics and language learning at the upper primary level. The proposal to assess preschoolers using neuroscience methods was explicitly rejected.

results.title: Breakdown of Applicants for Tamil Language Teaching Positions by Citizenship Status in Past Five Years
results.summary: Over the past five years, the Ministry of Education averaged 750 annual Tamil Language teaching applications, with 80% from local applicants (Singapore citizens and permanent residents) and 20% from foreigners. Local applicants achieved a 4% recruitment success rate, while foreign applicants achieved 2%, reflecting a prioritisation of local hiring. Foreign recruitment occurs only to supplement the local workforce when local recruitment targets are not met, with additional consideration given to assimilation ability.

results.title: Breakdown of Bullying Incidents and Awareness of New Mandatory Bully-reporting Channels
results.summary: The Minister for Education directed members to the oral reply transcript dated 23 September 2025 for data on bullying incident rates, counselling referrals, absenteeism, and suicide-risk referrals from 2020 to date. That same reply addressed the new mandatory bully-reporting channels under the enhanced discipline framework, including standardisation, age-appropriateness, communication strategies, and safeguards for reporters. The transcript does not contain the specific statistical figures or detailed operational metrics requested in the questions.

results.title: Breakdown of Civil Service and Statutory Boards Recruitment by Employment Type in Past Five Years
results.summary: Between 2020 and 2024, the Public Service recruited approximately 14,000 officers annually, with about 76% appointed on contracts-of-service. Contract roles serve specific projects or assess suitability for permanent careers, while permanent appointments are reserved for roles requiring extended training, such as in education or uniformed services. All new hires undergo a probation period of at least one year before confirmation. For 2020 and 2021 contract recruits, roughly 45% were emplaced, 28% exited within two years, and the remainder were considered for later emplacement, exited, or assigned to finite-tenure projects. The Public Service Division does not track the proportion of emplaced officers in Statutory Boards.

results.title: Breakdown of Premature Live Births in Restructured Hospitals by Gestational Age Bands for Last Five Years
results.summary: Over the last five years, premature live births (defined as <37 weeks gestation) in restructured hospitals—KKH, SGH, and NUH—are detailed in Table 1. The average length of stay for these newborns in Neonatal Intensive Care Units ranges from 154 to 299 days. Specific statistics for each year and gestational age band, as well as the median and 90th-percentile stay durations, are provided in the referenced table.

results.title: Breakdown of Recruitment Fees Paid by Work Permit Holders in Singapore and in Home Countries
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower conducts ad hoc surveys on recruitment fees for first-time non-domestic work permit holders in 2024, revealing typical ranges of $600 to $7,800 across sectors like construction and manufacturing. Fees vary by gender, with males typically paying between $1,000 and $7,200 compared to $300 to $5,600 for females. The Ministry does not publicly release data broken down by nationality due to bilateral sensitivities and community considerations. While the Ministry performs these surveys, it has not released findings with specific breakdowns by country of origin, sector, or gender beyond the provided typical ranges.

results.title: Breakdown of Savings, Withdrawals and Closing Balances of CDA Accounts by Household Income Decile Groups for Children Born between 2014 and 2017
results.summary: For children born between 2014 and 2017, the Child Development Account (CDA) closes upon turning 12, not seven as initially queried. From 2016 to 2024, over 85% of CDA withdrawals were for educational expenses, 10% for healthcare, and 5% for other approved uses. For the 2008 to 2012 birth cohorts, the average private savings excluding government contributions were $10,200, total withdrawals averaged $12,500, and closing balances were $3,000. Specific breakdowns by household income decile groups for the 2014 to 2017 cohorts are unavailable because the Ministry of Social and Family Development does not mandate the collection of parents' income data for the Baby Bonus scheme.

results.title: Challenges of and Measures to Ensure Successful Recruitment of Tamil Language Teaching Positions
results.summary: The Minister for Education outlined a multi-pronged strategy to strengthen the Tamil Language teaching recruitment pipeline. Measures include sparking early student interest, identifying older students with strong skills via Education and Career Guidance for teaching internships, and engaging undergraduates, fresh graduates, and mid-career professionals. Suitable candidates from the Tamil Language Elective Programme may participate in the Teaching Internship Programme before pursuing relevant degrees. Additionally, the Ministry proactively recruits potential teachers who will be sponsored for training at the National Institute of Education to become qualified Tamil Language teachers. The response focused on recruitment pathways and did not detail specific challenges hindering application conversion.

results.title: Clarifying Regulations Restricting Employed Delivery Riders from Undertaking Food and Parcel Deliveries
results.summary: The Minister clarified that no regulations restrict Singaporeans or Permanent Residents from undertaking food and parcel delivery work as employees or platform workers. Foreigners are permitted to perform such work only within an employer-employee relationship holding a valid work pass, as the Ministry does not issue passes for platform work. To address illegal activities, the Platform Workers Trilateral Group issued recommendations for strengthened enforcement and stringent penalties against individuals performing illegal work or abetting it. While legitimate workers and businesses will not be penalised, increased enforcement checks may temporarily inconvenience some entities. The Ministry appreciates local delivery riders' assistance in these checks and seeks their cooperation to deter illegal platform sector activities.

results.title: Collection of Official Fees for Honorary Consuls-General and Honorary Consuls
results.summary: Honorary Consuls-General and Honorary Consuls are appointed by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MFA) to advance Singapore's interests without compensation, bearing all operational costs themselves. Their letters of appointment stipulate that they may retain collected official fees to offset these expenditures. The MFA annually informs them of the applicable fee rates under the Diplomatic and Consular Officers (Fees) Order 2012, which must be publicly posted for public verification. While the Minister confirmed that accounting for these fees will be regularised, she did not specify a timeline for this process or confirm if signed agreements exist for fee retention.

results.title: Considerations for Expansion of No-smoking Buffer Zones from Five to Ten Metres
results.summary: The Ministry for Sustainability and the Environment confirmed there are no plans to expand the current five-metre no-smoking buffer zones to ten metres, as the existing distance aligns with local and international standards for ambient air intake. Smoking remains restricted to clearly marked Designated Smoking Areas within prohibited zones like Orchard Road, while other areas such as covered walkways and playgrounds maintain total bans. The Ministry will continue to review smoking prohibition policies periodically but has not adopted the Tokyo model of restricting smoking solely to designated areas.

results.title: Considerations when Planning for Community Clubs with Mass Facilities
results.summary: The People's Association plans each Community Club (CC) to serve approximately 15,000 households or 50,000 residents, with site selection based on population growth, geography, and long-term needs. While barring new towns under development, the PA ensures access to CCs, Integrated Community Hubs, or Residents' Network Centres within a reasonable radius for all residents. Upcoming multi-facility developments include the Bukit Timah Integrated Development, Heart of Yew Tee, and Tampines Boulevard Community Club. The Minister noted that alternative facilities like Residents' Network Centres are established in divisions lacking a CC to provide immediate community touchpoints.

results.title: Data on Change of Employer Letters and In-principle Approvals Issued between 1 January 2025 to 30 June 2025
results.summary: Approximately 1,600 Work Permit holders with valid salary claims received Change of Employer (COE) letters in 2024, rising to 1,100 between January and June 2025. Of these recipients, about 65% subsequently secured In-Principle Approvals within the COE validity period. The remaining 35% either did not submit applications or faced rejection due to employer non-compliance with requirements, such as insufficient Work Permit quotas. These figures reflect the operational constraints and success rates for work permit transfers under current regulations.

results.title: Data on Rejected LTVP and LTVP+ Applications from 2020 to 2024
results.summary: From 2020 to 2024, an average of approximately 600 Long-Term Visit Pass (LTVP) or LTVP+ applications were rejected annually for foreign spouses of Singapore citizens married for at least three years. Among these rejected applicants, roughly 3% reported having at least one Singaporean child. The Coordinating Minister for National Security and Minister for Home Affairs provided these statistics without detailing the specific grounds for rejection or outlining any subsequent policy changes regarding these applications.

results.title: Data on Secondhand Smoke Dispute Cases between Neighbours in Public and Private Estates
results.summary: The National Environment Agency tracks feedback on second-hand tobacco smoke complaints from neighbours but does not record the number of formal disputes between neighbours in public or private residential properties. Consequently, specific data on dispute volumes is not available for public release. Dispute resolution processes for these cases are not detailed in the provided transcript.

results.title: Data on Unsuccessful Applicants of Phase 2C Supplementary Primary One Registration Exercise and Engaging Parents in School Allocation
results.summary: In the 2025 Primary 1 Registration Exercise, approximately 400 applicants (1% of total registrants) remained unplaced after Phase 2C Supplementary. The Ministry of Education (MOE) assists these families by allocating their children to nearby schools with available vacancies, typically within two kilometres of their residential address. Parents are encouraged to contact the school or MOE directly for any queries regarding these allocations.

results.title: Declaration of ASEAN Day When Assuming ASEAN Chairmanship in 2027 and Other Related Initiatives
results.summary: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs will spearhead whole-of-Government efforts in 2027 to plan commemorative events for Singapore's ASEAN Chairmanship, coinciding with ASEAN's 60th anniversary. Initiatives will include declaring ASEAN Day and encouraging regional travel to strengthen regional identity and connectivity. The Ministry aims to raise awareness of ASEAN's importance among Singaporeans, particularly the youth, through activities such as the ASEAN Tapestry Programme. These measures are designed to befitting the diamond jubilee and promote regional unity.

results.title: Defining Criteria and Management Regulations for Home-based Businesses
results.summary: The Home-Based Business (HBB) scheme permits small-scale residential activities provided they do not alter the property's character, impact neighbours, employ outsiders, or display signage. The Urban Redevelopment Authority and Housing and Development Board monitor compliance, requiring operators to scale down or relocate if breaches occur. Liquor licences from the Singapore Police Force are mandatory for onsite alcohol supply, while food and beverage operators remain subject to the Environmental Public Health Act and Sale of Food Act despite lacking specific food agency licensing. All HBB income is taxable under standard rules without distinction from other businesses, enforced through regular Inland Revenue Authority of Singapore audits and public reporting channels for suspected non-compliance.

results.title: Deploying Polder Approach to Protect East Coast and North-West Coastal Areas Against Rising Sea Levels
results.summary: The Government will monitor the Pulau Tekong polder pilot to evaluate the viability of deploying the empoldering approach for future East Coast and North-West coastal protection against rising sea levels. For existing reclamation projects, the Housing and Development Board completes upfront soil improvement works to ensure reclaimed land is safe for building development. No specific resting period before construction was stated; safety depends on completed soil improvement works. The decision to adopt this method for new coastal areas remains under review based on pilot insights.

results.title: Details on Proposed Collaboration Between SPH Media and Chinese State-owned News Organisations
results.summary: The Minister clarified that SPH Media's collaborations with Chinese state-owned news organisations are standard for international coverage and do not inherently raise concerns. She affirmed that existing regulations under the Newspaper and Printing Presses Act and the Broadcasting Act safeguard against foreign interference in editorial content and decision-making. Specifically, funds from foreign sources for non-commercial purposes are prohibited for local newspaper and broadcasting companies. These legal safeguards apply regardless of government funding status, with swift action pledged against any detected interference.

results.title: Development of CARA Mobile App for Caregivers of Persons with Disabilities or Children with Autism to Report Missing Persons
results.summary: The CARA mobile app, co-developed by Dementia Singapore, AIC, and NCSS, enables caregivers of persons with dementia to report missing loved ones via DementiaHub.SG. For children with autism or persons with special needs, the Ministry does not include this reporting feature in CARA; instead, caregivers must report missing persons to the Police. These individuals should hold a Developmental Disability Registry (DDR) Identity Card, which displays caregiver and agency contact details to facilitate the person's return. The app and DDR card address the distinct needs of their respective target groups.

results.title: Differentiated Property Tax Rates for Vacant and Non-vacant Commercial and Industrial Properties
results.summary: The Government has not studied differentiated property tax rates for vacant commercial and industrial properties, as vacancy rates have remained stable and comparable to pre-COVID levels over the past three years. Consequently, there is no basis to introduce such rates, which are uncommon globally. The Minister noted that factors like rentals, location, economic conditions, and market demand significantly influence leasing decisions, making the effectiveness of differentiated tax structures unclear. Therefore, the Government will not consider implementing differentiated rates to reduce vacancy rates in this sector.

results.title: Effectiveness of Measures to Prevent and Deter Scams
results.summary: In the first half of 2025, scam cases decreased by 26% and losses fell by 13% compared to the same period in 2024, attributed to existing anti-scam initiatives. Despite these improvements, $456.4 million was still lost, with 79% of incidents involving self-effected transfers where victims were manipulated into moving funds. The Minister highlighted that the ultimate defence remains an alert public urged to verify requests by calling 1799 before responding. Further measures are not explicitly detailed beyond the reference to the Mid-Year Scam and Cybercrime Brief 2025, which outlines recently implemented and upcoming initiatives. The government acknowledges that scammers will continue to pivot, necessitating ongoing vigilance from citizens to prevent future losses.

results.title: Efforts to Educate and Protect Migrant Workers against Kickbacks
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) regularly educates migrant workers on kickbacks through mandatory Settling-In-Programmes, the FWMOMCare app, social media, and NGO partnerships. Under the Employment of Foreign Manpower Act, offenders face up to two years' imprisonment, fines of up to $30,000, or both, with courts imposing substantial fines of $10,000 to $15,000 per charge and ordering restitution. MOM facilitates employment changes for workers reporting kickbacks without fear of reprisal, maintaining low case numbers. The Minister stated that penalties will be reviewed where necessary, though no specific legislative amendments or increased education frequency were proposed in this session.

results.title: Eligibility Criteria for Emissions-Intensive Trade-Exposed Companies to Claim Transitional Allowances
results.summary: The Minister confirmed that transitional allowances support Emissions-Intensive Trade-Exposed (EITE) companies competing globally against jurisdictions with lower carbon prices. Eligibility and allowance levels will be reviewed and adjusted based on companies' decarbonisation plan delivery, international developments, and technological advancements. No specific timeline or targeted end date for the transition framework was stated. The provision of allowances remains subject to these ongoing reviews rather than a fixed schedule.

results.title: Enforcement Measures Against Cyclists' and Riders' Usage of Pedestrian-only Paths
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority (LTA) employs a dual approach combining education campaigns with active enforcement to manage pedestrian-only paths (POPs). Since August 2024, enforcement officers have educated users ahead of the POP rule's commencement on 1 July 2025. POPs feature clear markings to guide users. Warnings are issued first, followed by fines for intentional disobedience or errant riding that endangers others. The LTA does not yet specify measures to reduce conflicts on narrow paths where users inadvertently stray into each other's lanes.

results.title: Enforcing against Illegal Ride-hailing
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport directed that the question regarding illegal ride-hailing trends and enforcement actions be addressed via a written reply for Question No 35 at the 23 September 2025 Parliament Sitting. No specific statistics on the increase in illegal ride-hailing or details of further enforcement measures were provided in this transcript. The response explicitly defers to the written record for the requested data and action plans.

results.title: Ensuring Aviation and Aerospace Sectors Continue to be Attractive to Singaporean Workers
results.summary: To sustain growth in the aviation and aerospace sectors, which currently employ over 80,000 workers, the Ministry is investing in Terminal 5, the Changi East industrial zone, and new aerospace anchors. Tripartite partners are advancing three key measures: promoting job redesign and technology adoption, including an August 2024 pilot between SATS and NTUC; expanding upskilling via the Career Conversion Programme, which has benefited over 5,000 individuals as of 2024; and strengthening recruitment through partnerships with higher learning institutions and sector-wide fairs like the OneAviation event scheduled for July 2025. Additionally, CAAS has signed a memorandum of understanding with AI Singapore to accelerate artificial intelligence capability building across the industry.

results.title: Environmental Impact Assessments (EIA) Requiring Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDAs) from 2021 to 2025 and Possible Publication of Clearer Guidelines for EIA NDAs
results.summary: Most Environmental Impact Assessments (EIA) reports are publicly available online by default. For cases involving confidentiality, such as national security, agencies provide hardcopy reports to interested parties upon request, requiring viewers to sign Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDAs). The Minister clarified that NDAs are applied only when specific confidentiality considerations exist, rather than as a standard requirement for all EIAs. The response did not address the proportion of EIAs requiring NDAs in the last five years or confirm the publication of clearer guidelines defining exceptional conditions.

results.title: Evacuation Procedures for High-rise Fires in Older HDB Blocks
results.summary: The Singapore Civil Defence Force (SCDF) prioritises evacuating occupants on fire-affected floors and the two floors above, while residents in unaffected units should remain inside unless SCDF deems evacuation necessary. This approach aligns with international best practices to minimise injuries. SCDF constantly reviews its procedures but currently has no plans to mandate smoke curtains or evacuation chairs in HDB blocks, citing limited additional benefits over existing measures. The agency will continue assessing other technologies that may enhance operations.

results.title: Exempted Business
results.summary: The Deputy Speaker granted consent to extend today's proceedings on exempted business beyond the scheduled interruption time. The Leader of the House sought and received general assent from Members present to move this motion. No specific policy measures, timelines, or eligibility rules regarding exempted business were discussed in this segment. The transcript contains only procedural details regarding the extension of debate time without substantive policy content.

results.title: Expanding Scope of MyResponder App to Include Alerts for HDB Fires and Missing Persons with Disabilities
results.summary: The Minister for Home Affairs stated there are no plans to expand the myResponder app to include alerts for missing persons, noting that missing person cases require different capabilities than the app's current function for minor fires and medical cases. The Police maintain an established process for locating missing persons, utilizing public appeals published through various channels including social media. Consequently, the app will continue to alert Community First Responders only to nearby minor fires and medical cases, without adding features for missing persons or HDB fire alerts.

results.title: Expanding and Upgrading Solar Energy Transmission and Distribution Infrastructure by 2030
results.summary: The Energy Market Authority collaborates with SP Group to annually review grid network plans, including necessary upgrades to transmission and distribution infrastructure and energy storage systems to support solar deployment. These measures ensure the infrastructure keeps pace with the target of achieving at least two gigawatt-peak (GWp) of solar energy by 2030. SP Group is currently executing these plans to mitigate solar intermittency and accommodate domestic solar growth.

results.title: Expertise and Technological Expertise of Building Industry and Contractors to Solve Water Seepage in HDB Flats
results.summary: The Minister confirmed that the Building and Construction Authority (BCA) and industry co-developed a Waterproofing Accreditation Scheme to establish competency standards for waterproofing specialists. To address complex water seepage in aging HDB flats, HDB supports Town Councils through joint inspections, technical advice, and repair recommendations. Recognizing the need for advanced solutions amid extreme weather, BCA and HDB will deepen partnerships with the research community to scale effective technologies, including microwave scanning to detect seepage sources within concrete. The Ministry aims to further equip contractors with necessary expertise and capabilities to handle increasing challenges.

results.title: Factors Leading to Reduction of MRT Reliability between June 2024 and June 2025
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport stated that questions regarding systemic issues in MRT reliability reduction between June 2024 and June 2025, collaboration between LTA and rail operators, and funding for reliability upgrades to avoid passing costs to commuters have been addressed in Parliamentary Questions Nos 19 to 24 at the 22 September 2025 Parliament Sitting. No further details on specific measures, timelines, or operational constraints were provided in this response.

results.title: Factors to Determine Success of AV Trials in Punggol
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport deferred the response regarding AV trial success criteria, safety measures, and insurance liability in Punggol. He stated these questions will be addressed at the next Sitting on 25 September 2025, alongside similar Parliamentary Questions, to provide a more holistic reply. No specific criteria, measures, or insurance arrangements were detailed in this session.

results.title: Formal Recognition of Palestinian State, Upholding Two-state Solution and Assessing Future Singapore-Israel Relations
results.summary: The Minister for Foreign Affairs and Senior Minister of State for Foreign Affairs addressed the question regarding Palestinian state recognition and Singapore-Israel relations in Ministerial Statements delivered on 22 September 2025. These statements covered the situation in the Middle East, including humanitarian law breaches in Gaza, allegations of genocide, and violations of Qatar's sovereignty. The transcript does not contain the specific Government positions on formally recognising a Palestinian state, upholding the two-state solution, or the future assessment of Singapore-Israel relations. Consequently, the detailed answers to the parliamentary questions are not available in the provided text.

results.title: Funding Criteria for Graduate Degree Programmes Government Subsidies
results.summary: The Ministry of Education (MOE) limits post-graduate subsidies to specific Master's programmes aligned with industry needs or serving as entry requirements for professions like architecture, determined through sector agency consultation. While MOE regularly reviews this subsidy framework, Autonomous Universities may offer non-subsidised programmes if deemed viable. Beyond MOE subsidies, most universities provide fee rebates to Singaporeans, Permanent Residents, and alumni, alongside additional scholarships and study awards for eligible students. The Ministry will continue to assess programme viability and relevance to national manpower needs.

results.title: Grants for Lower-income PMD Owners to Offset Battery Replacement Costs to Reduce Fire Risks
results.summary: No new grants are proposed for lower-income PMD owners to offset battery replacement costs, as existing support is restricted to LTA-compliant suppliers to mitigate fire risks. Instead, eligible persons with disabilities and seniors aged 60 and above may apply for subsidised Personal Mobility Aids (PMAs) via SG Enable or the Agency for Integrated Care under the Assistive Technology Fund and Seniors' Mobility and Enabling Fund. For lower-income PMD owners receiving ComCare, cash assistance is available to help defray some battery replacement costs.

results.title: Grid Connection Capacity as Basis for Allocating Access to Electricity Import Landing Sites and Other Strategic Considerations
results.summary: The allocation of electricity import landing sites depends on project locations, development timelines, and infrastructure planning considerations including land, sea corridor space, and grid availability. The Minister stated that available grid connection capacity is not the sole basis for allocation. Strategic factors such as emissions intensity, system resilience, and long-term sustainability were not explicitly confirmed as incorporated into the current decision-making process.

results.title: Guidelines Encouraging Responsible Use of Artificial Intelligence in Schools
results.summary: The Ministry of Education has issued broad guidelines to all schools and Institutes of Higher Learning regarding artificial intelligence (AI) use, effective immediately, which will evolve over time. These guidelines mandate that AI use in assessment must align with learning outcomes and is prohibited for tasks requiring independent mastery, such as in-class assignments, school-based assessments, and proctored national examinations. Conversely, educators may design homework allowing AI use with proper acknowledgment, provided they specify usage rules and explain decisions to students. The Ministry introduced the AI in Education Ethics Framework in 2022 to guide tool development, while lower primary levels prioritize concrete, hands-on learning before gradually introducing guided AI use under teacher supervision from middle primary onwards. Each institution maintains its own policies and disciplinary frameworks to address AI misuse and uphold academic integrity.

results.title: HDB Shop Rental Trends and Efforts to Keep Rents Affordable
results.summary: As of August 2025, HDB manages approximately 7,000 directly rented shops and 8,500 sold units, with direct rents rising moderately at 1.3% to 3.3% annually over the past three years. To ensure affordability, HDB applies the Price-Quality Method (PQM) tender for new neighbourhood centres and GP clinics, assigning 60% to 70% of points to operator quality, which successfully lowered GP clinic rents in pilot cases. While HDB does not currently collect sublet rent data, it monitors market trends and may selectively acquire privately held shops if supply is insufficient for essential services like food and medical care. Future PQM implementation will likely extend to more clinics, though acquiring already high-priced sold shops remains unlikely due to fiscal constraints.

results.title: Impact of Carbon Tax Increase on Emissions Produced in Singapore and Household Utilities Costs
results.summary: The Minister for Sustainability and the Environment directed Members to a written reply dated 22 September 2025 regarding the Carbon Tax. This document addresses the planned tax increases, their projected impact on Singapore's emissions, and effects on household utility costs. Specific details on whether the 2028 increase will be pushed back or reduced were not provided in the transcript. Further reference to the written reply is required for complete information on these measures.

results.title: Impact of Increased Volatility in GIC's Returns on Interest Rates of Special Singapore Government Securities
results.summary: Interest rates on Special Singapore Government Securities (SSGS) are determined by CPF interest rates and remain independent of GIC's returns or volatility. The Government guarantees SSGS interest payments to the CPF Board regardless of GIC's performance, as CPF savings are invested exclusively in these risk-free securities. This guarantee is underpinned by a prudent fiscal policy that maintains a healthy net asset buffer to absorb market risks across all cycles. Consequently, fluctuations in GIC's returns over the next decade will not impact the interest rates paid on SSGS.

results.title: Impact of US GENIUS Act on Regulated Digital Assets and Recognition of Stablecoin Regulatory Framework
results.summary: The US Senate passed the GENIUS Act in July to regulate stablecoins, with rule-making underway for implementation by January 2027. While adoption could spur global efficiency, risks like value instability and currency substitution require robust global regulatory guidance. MAS published its stablecoin framework in 2023 to ensure value stability, distinguishing regulated stablecoins from high-risk crypto assets. The authority is formalising this framework via legislative amendments and a public consultation later this year. MAS actively monitors US and European developments, considering regulatory cooperation for safe cross-border stablecoin use. Simultaneously, MAS supports industry innovation in distributed ledger technologies, with key financial institutions establishing digital asset centres of excellence and testing tokenised financial instruments for cross-border payments and programmable rewards.

results.title: Impact of Unused Carbon Offset Limits from 2024 to 2025 on Singapore’s Carbon Emissions Pathway
results.summary: The Government permitted companies to roll over unused carbon offset limits from 2024 to 2025 due to constrained International Carbon Credit supply in a nascent market. This measure was not explicitly linked to Singapore's emissions pathway projection in the statement. The Ministry has not yet decided whether to extend this rollover provision for Emission Year 2025, despite the carbon tax rising to $45/tCO2 in 2026. The continuation of the policy remains undecided pending further review.

results.title: Implementation of Time-based Limits for Learning Purposes at Early Childhood Development Centres
results.summary: The Early Childhood Development Agency (ECDA) does not implement strict time-based limits on screen use for learning at Early Childhood Development Centres. Instead, the Code of Practice mandates no screen use for infants up to 18 months and restricts older children (18 months to six years) to teaching and learning purposes only. This approach prioritizes purposeful digital tool usage to foster interaction and engagement rather than mere occupation. The policy aims to support families in building balanced technology relationships without specifying a separate implementation timeline, as requirements are already outlined in existing guidelines.

results.title: Improving Financial Governance and Policy Decision-making Using AI and Data Analytics
results.summary: The Minister for Finance stated that the question regarding the Ministry's use of AI and data analytics for financial governance, fraud detection, and policy decision-making was previously addressed in a Written Reply. That response was provided to Parliamentary Question No 61 by Mr Xie Yao Quan for oral answer on 23 September 2025. No new details, timelines, or specific measures were introduced during this session. The matter remains covered by the existing written record.

results.title: Improving Student Suicide Prevention Efforts and Evaluation of “Be A Samaritan” Programme
results.summary: The Ministry of Education collaborates with partners like the Ministry of Health, Institute of Mental Health, and Samaritans of Singapore to strengthen early identification and referral of at-risk students through multi-faceted strategies. Relevant agencies provide helplines such as Mindline 1771 and conduct awareness training, including a June 2025 Learning Journey where educators were introduced to the "Be A Samaritan" programme. The Minister stated that while these efforts aim to improve peer referrals and help-seeking rates, it is not feasible to evaluate each individual programme separately due to the diverse nature of the suicide prevention ecosystem. Consequently, specific behavioural outcome data for the "Be A Samaritan" and "Be A Samaritan Junior" programmes were not provided in the response.

results.title: Increase of Appeals on 15-month HDB Purchase Wait-out Period by Private Property Downgraders from 2022 to 2025
results.summary: The Minister for National Development stated that the question regarding the increase in appeals for waiving the 15-month HDB wait-out period for private property downgraders since 2022 has already been addressed in the reply to Parliamentary Question No 112. Additionally, the Minister confirmed that the outcome of the review concerning the wait-out period will be released soon. No further details on specific appeal numbers or the exact timeline for the review outcome were provided in this transcript.

results.title: Infrastructure Developments and Legacy Plans to Promote Para-community Sports Events and Inclusivity
results.summary: Sport Singapore collaborates with partners to ensure competition venues meet international accessibility standards, exemplified by the OCBC Aquatic Centre's step-free access and dedicated wheelchair bays for the 2025 World Para Swimming Championships. The refreshed 2024 Disability Sports Master Plan aims to increase accessibility, enhance awareness, and support high-performance aspirations through the new Enabling Sports Fund. Mainstream schools will conduct disability sports programmes by 2028 to foster empathy, while facility upgrades include increasing wheelchair-accessible ramps in public swimming pools from six in 2023 to ten by end-2025. Additionally, all ActiveSG gyms are targeted to become fully inclusive by 2026, reflecting a steady enhancement of accessibility across facilities to encourage participation by persons with disabilities.

results.title: Initiatives and Measures to Raise Public Awareness on Correct Usage of Respective Paths among Pedestrians and Cyclists
results.summary: LTA Active Mobility Enforcement Officers conduct regular patrols to enforce path segregation between cycling and pedestrian-only paths. To enhance safety, LTA collaborates with relevant agencies to install convex mirrors at locations with line-of-sight issues and trim overgrown hedges that narrow paths. The Authority will continue raising awareness through education campaigns, community partnerships, and direct user education to encourage adherence to designated path portions. No specific timeline or new legislation was announced regarding these enforcement and safety measures.

results.title: Initiatives and Projects towards Attaining 2030 Target of 75% Mass Public Transport Modal Share
results.summary: The peak-period mass public transport modal share was 66% last year, with a target of 75% by 2030. The Ministry plans to achieve this once the Jurong Region Line and Cross Island Line are completed in the early 2030s, which will place eight in 10 households within a 10-minute walk of a train station. Concurrently, the Bus Connectivity Enhancement Programme aims to improve bus services for residents farther from transport nodes to enhance overall public transport attractiveness.

results.title: Installation of One Fire Extinguisher at Lift Lobby of Every Two HDB Blocks
results.summary: Since October 2023, the Singapore Civil Defence Force, HDB, and Town Councils have installed fire extinguishers at lift lobbies of one in every two residential HDB blocks under a joint initiative with Temasek Foundation. More than 90% of targeted blocks are now equipped, with the roll-out completion targeted for 2028. To date, over 220 extinguishers have been used at minor fire incidents. The Ministry is currently focused on finishing the installation for remaining blocks and will assess future needs after completion.

results.title: Investigations into Pulau Tekong BMTC Fires and Measures to Improve Fire Safety
results.summary: The Singapore Civil Defence Force completed investigations into two fires at the Basic Military Training Centre (BMTC) on Pulau Tekong on 20 June 2025 and 11 July 2025, concluding that naked flames applied to combustible materials were the ignition source in both unoccupied accommodation blocks with no injuries. Following these incidents, the Ministry and Singapore Armed Force have increased patrols around unoccupied buildings and plan to install closed-circuit television cameras for added security. BMTC buildings continue to comply with national fire safety standards, undergo routine audits by certified inspectors, and conduct regular drills, including one for all recruits within 48 hours of enlistment, while maintaining high emergency readiness through close collaboration with SCDF.

results.title: Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone as Part of Singapore's Overall Effort to Attract Foreign Direct Investments
results.summary: The Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ) leverages Singapore's global connectivity and Johor's land advantages to attract foreign direct investment. In April 2025, the Ministry of Trade and Industry, Singapore Economic Development Board, and EnterpriseSG established a dedicated JS-SEZ Project Office to support Singapore-based companies expanding into the zone. This office collaborates with Malaysian authorities on investment promotion, including a Joint Investment Forum in Johor in April 2025 and a subsequent forum in Singapore later that year. The government is also exploring partnerships with private sector developers building new industrial parks to better support Singapore's strategic industry transformation interests.

results.title: Lessons from Fire at Children's Enrichment Centre at River Valley Road Shophouse in April 2025
results.summary: Investigations into the April 8, 2025 fire at the River Valley Road shophouse are ongoing. Preliminary findings by the Singapore Civil Defence Force (SCDF) indicate the fire likely originated from a second-storey storage area and identified non-compliances, including unauthorised partition erection. The Minister for Home Affairs noted these safety breaches but did not yet specify the exact cause or confirm if lessons learned have been formalized into new regulations for children's enrichment centres or general shophouse premises.

results.title: Management Plans of Long-term Community Animals
results.summary: The Ministry for National Development implements the Trap-Neuter-Rehome/Release Manage (TNRM) programmes for free-roaming dogs and community cats, launched in 2018 and 2024 respectively. In partnership with animal welfare groups, veterinarians, and the community, these animals are humanely captured, sterilised, microchipped, and ear-tipped before being rehomed or released. The National Parks Board will continue to support the TNRM programme.

results.title: Managing Commercial Rentals given Rising Costs and High Churn in F&B Sector
results.summary: The Deputy Prime Minister and Minister for Trade and Industry noted that aggregate private retail rent increases have trended below inflation over the past three years, though local variations exist based on location and property attributes. To assist businesses in managing costs, the Government introduced a 50% Corporate Income Tax rebate for Years of Assessment 2024 and 2025, capped at $40,000 with a minimum benefit of $2,000 for companies employing at least one local worker. Enterprise Singapore has launched initiatives like FoodX to help food and beverage firms outsource food preparation to central kitchens, thereby streamlining operations and reducing manpower intensity. Additional support includes schemes for retailers adopting experiential concepts or productivity-enhancing formats such as self-checkout, alongside CDC Vouchers and SG60 vouchers for eligible hawkers and heartland businesses to boost patronage.

results.title: Measures to Boost and Improve Paper and Domestic Recycling Rate
results.summary: The Minister for Sustainability and the Environment stated that the question regarding new measures to boost paper and domestic recycling rates has already been addressed in a written response to Question No 20 on 23 September 2025. No additional new measures, timelines, or specific operational constraints were introduced during this parliamentary session. The Ministry directs interested parties to refer to the official written answer for the full details on the current recycling rate improvement strategies and eligibility conditions.

results.title: Measures to Diversify Food Imports and Moderate Food Prices in View of Volatile Global Food Prices
results.summary: Singapore cannot fully insulate itself from global food disruptions as it imports over 90% of its food. To mitigate risks, the government employs a multi-pronged strategy centered on import diversification, complemented by stockpiling and local production. Regionally, Singapore participates in the ASEAN Plus Three Emergency Rice Reserve (APTERR) to earmark rice for emergencies. Bilaterally, the Ministry of Trade and Industry facilitates trade negotiations and secured exemptions during recent non-Basmati white rice export restrictions. The approach also involves industry continuity planning and consumer flexibility. While the Minister outlined these existing measures, the specific proposal for regional joint stockpiling beyond APTERR or expanding community food cooperatives with ASEAN partners was not addressed in the response.

results.title: Measures to Ensure Fair Internship Compensation for Polytechnic, ITE and University Students
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that Institutes of Higher Learning (IHLs) ensure fair internship compensation by collaborating with companies to define job scopes and providing recommended allowance ranges based on industry norms. Key terms, including monthly allowances and roles, are mutually agreed upon between interns and employers before commencement, with students informed of these terms and support channels prior to starting. For credit-bearing internships, which constitute the majority for polytechnic and ITE students, IHLs assign officers to monitor learning and well-being while closely tracking feedback to maintain quality. Companies providing poor internship experiences have been barred from participating, ensuring that all internships remain relevant to students' courses of study.

results.title: Measures to Support Students' Voluntary Give Up of Vaping and Accessing Rehabilitation
results.summary: The Minister for Education directed that the question regarding strategies to support students voluntarily quitting vaping, accessing rehabilitation, or those arrested for vape offences, along with teacher training requirements, was previously addressed via a written answer on 23 September 2025. Members are advised to refer to the response to Questions Nos 108 and 109 in the 23 September 2025 Order Paper for the specific details. No new measures, timelines, or operational constraints were announced during this parliamentary session.

results.title: Mindfulness and Other Techniques Taught in Schools for Students' Mental and Emotional Wellness
results.summary: The Minister for Education confirmed that while mindfulness is not part of the curriculum, the Character and Citizenship Education (CCE) programme from primary to pre-university levels teaches social-emotional competencies like self-awareness and emotional regulation. CCE lessons cover coping strategies, managing negative thoughts, and help-seeking, with relevant practices such as breathing exercises and calming techniques integrated into instruction. These skills are further reinforced outside the classroom through outdoor adventure camps, co-curricular activities, and daily school interactions where students apply knowledge in real-life situations.

results.title: Monthly Average Electricity and Water Consumption Rates in 2024 by Housing Type and Project Trends for Next Five Years
results.summary: In 2024, average monthly household electricity consumption ranged from 175.7 kWh for 1-Room/2-Room HDB flats to 1208.2 kWh for landed properties, while water usage varied from 8.9 m³ to 30.6 m³ across housing types. Private condominiums recorded 522.0 kWh for electricity and 13.4 m³ for water. The Minister stated that electricity consumption is projected to gradually increase over the next five years due to factors including the shift to induction cooking, higher air-conditioner usage, and greater electric vehicle adoption. Conversely, water consumption is expected to continue decreasing as households adopt more efficient fittings and appliances.

results.title: NEA Guidelines on Hawkers' Mandatory Participation in SEHC Operators’ Charitable and Discount Programmes
results.summary: The Minister for Sustainability and the Environment stated that the question regarding NEA guidelines on mandatory hawker participation in SEHC operators' charitable and discount programmes has already been addressed in an oral reply to Parliamentary Questions No 8 to 10 at the 24 September 2025 Parliament sitting. The response did not provide new details on whether such clauses are prohibited, the public availability of the guidelines, or the release of documents for public education. No further clarification on these specific points was offered during this session.

results.title: Number of Fires in HDB Estates Caused by Charging of AMDs
results.summary: Between 2020 and 2024, 98 fires occurred in HDB estates during the charging of Active Mobility Devices (AMDs). The Land Transport Authority (LTA) has intensified enforcement against non-compliant devices, requiring LTA approval for imports since June 2021 and mandating adherence to technical safety standards. While AMDs currently charge via standard wall sockets, LTA and relevant agencies will continue reviewing storage and charging guidelines. The Authority is also considering tougher enforcement actions to deter the sale or use of non-compliant devices that pose significant safety risks.

results.title: Ongoing Efforts and Measures to Monitor Rail Workers' Well-being and Safety during Repair Works
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport stated that questions regarding the monitoring of rail workers' work hours and safety measures during repair works following recent train disruptions have already been addressed. The specific details for these measures are contained in the official reply to Parliamentary Questions Nos 19 to 24, delivered during the 22 September 2025 Parliament sitting. No new information or additional operational constraints were provided in this session.

results.title: People's Association Policy to Grant Elected Opposition MPs Fair and Equitable Access to Community Centre Facilities within Their Own Constituencies
results.summary: The Minister for Culture, Community and Youth stated that the People's Association (PA) operates as a Statutory Board serving the Government of the day to foster social cohesion and implement Government policies. Consequently, PA facilities and events are strictly prohibited from being used for partisan political activities by any political party or for religious and unlawful purposes. While PA spaces are open to all residents regardless of constituency, background, or political affiliation, access is conditioned on adherence to this non-partisan mandate. The Government will appoint grassroots advisers to lead these organisations, ensuring they explain Government policies honestly and assist in governance without conducting political activities. This operational constraint ensures PA facilities serve the broader community while fulfilling their statutory role.

results.title: Percentage Breakdown of A Patient's Total Bill by Component
results.summary: The Ministry of Health established approximately 2,800 professional fee benchmarks covering about 2,200 surgical procedures and medical conditions to enable meaningful cost comparisons. Annual bill information is published with component breakdowns, including surgeon fees and operating facility charges, to assist patients in selecting healthcare providers. The Ministry will study expanding these fee benchmarks to guide private hospital fee setting further. Additionally, the Multilateral Healthcare Insurance Committee, comprising representatives from MOH, private hospitals, insurers, and medical professional associations, will continue exploring measures to manage private healthcare costs. Specific percentage breakdowns for total bills vary significantly based on patient circumstances and treatments.

results.title: Percentage of Privately-held HDB Shops with More than 30-year Remaining on Their Lease and Possible Initiative for HDB to Buy Them Back
results.summary: The Minister for National Development directed Members to refer to a combined reply issued on 24 September 2025 regarding HDB shop rents. This existing response addresses similar inquiries about privately held HDB shops with more than 30 years of lease remaining and potential buy-back initiatives. The transcript does not provide the specific percentage of such shops or confirm whether HDB will consider buying back these leases in appropriate cases to curate the trade mix.

results.title: Plans for Land Clearing Beyond Slated 10.4ha from 2026 and Ensuring Retention of Biodiversity at Northern Areas
results.summary: The Minister for National Development clarified that a reply regarding land clearing beyond the 10.4ha slated for removal from 2026 and biodiversity retention measures for the northern area was provided during the 23 September 2025 Sitting. This response addressed Ms Hany Soh's specific inquiry concerning mitigation strategies for the proposed Woodlands Gateway development. The transcript does not contain the detailed content of the mitigation measures or confirm if additional land clearing plans exist beyond the stated 10.4ha.

results.title: Plans for Pedestrain Overhead Bridges (POBs) Lift Installations within Bidadari Estate
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority (LTA) will consider retrofitting lifts to Pedestrian Overhead Bridge 53189 in Bidadari Estate during the next phase of its lift upgrading programme. This decision follows a review of senior population growth in the area, alongside proximity to transport nodes and amenities. The Authority continuously assesses existing bridges for retrofit needs based on these demographic and operational factors. No specific timeline for the upgrade was provided in the statement.

results.title: Plans for Phasing Out Hybrid Vehicles
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport confirmed there are no current plans to phase out hybrid vehicles. All new car registrations must be cleaner-energy models, including hybrids, from 2030, with a target for all cars to be cleaner-energy by 2040. The Ministry is monitoring adoption rates rather than setting a specific deadline for hybrid discontinuation. This clarification aims to provide business and consumer certainty regarding the vehicle transition timeline.

results.title: Plans for SG Enable Training Grant beyond FY2024 and Continuation of Support for Existing Training Providers
results.summary: The SG Enable Training Grant provides up to 95% course fee subsidies and training allowances for persons with disabilities. The next Call for Proposals is scheduled for the first quarter of 2026, targeting inclusive and mainstream training providers. Beyond the grant, the Enabling Academy collaborates with providers to expand accessible courses and offers complimentary workshops on disability inclusion. These initiatives aim to support professional development and inclusive course design without specifying new public grant calls beyond the current cycle.

results.title: Plans to Expand Dementia Go-To Point Network and Related Public Awareness Initiatives
results.summary: The Minister clarified that Dementia Go-To Points (GTPs) are designated safe-return locations specifically for persons living with dementia, where trained staff assist in reuniting lost individuals with families and provide educational resources. For persons with disabilities, caregivers can apply for a Developmental Disability Registry (DDR) Identity Card containing caregiver and agency details to facilitate public assistance if the individual becomes disoriented. The Minister noted that if a person with autism or special needs goes missing, caregivers must file a Police report, as these initiatives address the distinct needs of their respective target groups.

results.title: Popularity of Enhanced Travel Smart Journeys Initiative
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport reported that the Travel Smart Journeys (TSJ) initiative, enhanced in January 2025 with rebates up to 80% of fares and additional qualifying bus services, has benefited approximately 7,000 commuters. About eight in 10 users on the North-East Line adjusted their travel times to avoid peak hours, while others switched to buses. Despite these enhancements, the Minister noted that adoption rates remain low. Consequently, the Land Transport Authority intends to continue improving the programme to encourage more commuters to shift their travel timing or change their mode of transport. The current process requires prior enrolment via the SimplyGo app to accumulate and redeem points, a mechanism the Minister did not confirm would change.

results.title: Possibility of TADM Settlements Becoming Court Judgements
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) lacks the legal authority to convert Tripartite Alliance for Dispute Management (TADM) settlements into Court judgments, as this power resides with the Judiciary. However, parties may voluntarily register a settlement agreement with the District Court to make it enforceable as a Court order. If an employer defaults on a registered agreement, the worker can seek enforcement through the Courts; for unregistered settlements, the worker must first obtain a Court order via litigation before enforcing payment. MOM and tripartite partners facilitate the process by engaging defaulting employers and advising workers on enforcement steps. Penalties for errant employers include warnings, fines, debarment from work passes, and prosecution.

results.title: Post-scam Care and Recovery Support Measures for Victims of Scams
results.summary: The Minister for Home Affairs directed Members to refer to the response to Parliamentary Question No 56 for an oral answer delivered on 22 September 2025 regarding post-scam care and recovery support measures. This response details current provisions for victims of scams, including support groups and counselling services. No additional measures or timelines were stated in this specific exchange beyond the reference to the prior written answer.

results.title: Potential Dangers of Installing Bright, Flickering and Strobe Lights on AMDs
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport stated that while lights generally enhance safety, certain types on active mobility devices (AMDs) can endanger other users. Under the code of conduct for public path users, AMD users must use lights considerately and avoid shining them onto others' faces. The Land Transport Authority (LTA) will monitor light usage, gather public feedback, and review guidelines where necessary. Additionally, LTA will reinforce safe riding practices through public education campaigns and enforcement officers will educate path users on proper etiquette. No specific study on light dangers was confirmed, but the Ministry will consider regulating lights based on feedback and monitoring.

results.title: Processing Issues in Household Means Eligibility System
results.summary: Processing issues in the Household Means Eligibility System (HOMES) were discovered on 13 January 2025 after a public member reported that her business income was incorrectly assessed as zero. While subsidy levels and eligibility criteria are published on agency websites, independent self-verification is considered cumbersome for individuals. Consequently, affected cases have been reassessed, and agencies are actively notifying impacted individuals of the corrections. The Ministry of Health confirmed that queries regarding subsidy disbursement accuracy should be directed to respective service providers rather than relying on autonomous system checks.

results.title: Progress of Installing Green Roofs at Bus Stops
results.summary: Land Transport Authority (LTA), in collaboration with the National Parks Board, has installed green roofs at 200 bus stops to reduce ambient heat. Studies indicate these roofs lower temperatures by up to two degrees Celsius. LTA plans to expand the project to bus stops near residential estates, health institutions, parks, and major transport nodes, including Hougang. This rollout will be timed with the planned replacement and upgrading of bus stop shelters to accommodate necessary infrastructure provisions.

results.title: Projections on Impact of AI on PMETs and Interventions to Support Young Graduates at Risk of Early-career Chronic Underemployment
results.summary: The Minister for Manpower directed inquiries regarding AI's impact on PMET job displacement and new targeted interventions for at-risk young graduates to the reply to Question No 91 in the Order Paper dated 23 September 2025. No specific projections, statistics, or additional measures beyond existing SkillsFuture schemes and the GRaduate Industry Traineeships Programme were stated in this response. Details on the projected impact of artificial intelligence and planned support interventions remain pending in the referenced parliamentary document.

results.title: Protocol for Investigation and Release of Foreign Visitors Detained for Criminal Offences
results.summary: The two Italian swimmers were issued a 12-month conditional warning and an entry ban for shoplifting items worth approximately $150, following a framework applied uniformly to all offenders regardless of nationality. Consular access was granted per the Vienna Convention after the swimmers requested it, but such assistance does not influence the investigation or legal outcome, as diplomatic intervention had no bearing on the case. The Police and Attorney-General's Chambers determined the penalty based on offence severity, mitigating factors, and how similar cases were treated, noting that entry bans apply only to foreigners. This approach ensures consistent treatment while adhering to international consular obligations without compromising judicial independence.

results.title: Providing Guidance to Schools Permitted to Self-develop Enrolment Criteria
results.summary: The Ministry of Education provides broad guidelines for schools developing their own Computing elective enrolment criteria, suggesting factors like demonstrated interest and basic mathematics proficiency. Schools must also consider student profiles, timetabling constraints, and teacher availability. To expand access, a 2025 pilot programme allows students keen on "O" level Computing to travel to designated computing centre schools. The Ministry has not committed to issuing specific new guidance for self-developed criteria beyond these existing operational parameters.

results.title: Provision of Affordable Meals and Other Contractual Obligations at Socially-conscious Enterprise Hawker Centres
results.summary: The National Environment Agency (NEA) oversees Socially-conscious Enterprise Hawker Centres (SEHCs), requiring operators to provide at least one value meal per stall at $3.00–$3.50, with stallholders not expected to incur losses on these items. Since 2018, NEA has enforced guidelines capping stall hours, limiting breach charges, and capping rents at independently assessed values, while reviewing tenancy agreements before signing. Operators bear app development costs for customer discounts, while stallholders contribute via promotional discounts to drive footfall. The "Pay-It-Forward" charitable scheme at Bukit Canberra was removed from new agreements following stakeholder feedback. NEA maintains flexibility for operators on commercial terms beyond hard guidelines, provided they are transparent and agreed upon during tenancy signings.

results.title: Provision of Dynamic Assistance Packages Pegged to Inflation Indices or Automatic Top-ups of Family Savings Accounts
results.summary: The Government declined to introduce dynamic assistance packages pegged to inflation indices or automatic Family Savings Account top-ups, citing that automated mechanisms may be too blunt compared to targeted support. Instead, it strengthened structural schemes this year by raising long-term care subsidies, increasing ComCare rates, enhancing Silver Support payouts, and raising GST Voucher – Cash eligibility thresholds in August. Additionally, a $1.2 billion Assurance Package enhancement was announced at Budget 2025, including Community Development Council Vouchers and extra U-Save rebates. The Minister noted that one-off support is provided as necessary to manage immediate cost-of-living pressures.

results.title: Public Disclosure of Tenancy Agreements for Hawker Centres under SEHC Operators
results.summary: The Minister for Sustainability and the Environment stated that the question regarding mandating public disclosure of full tenancy agreements for hawker centres operated by Socially-conscious Enterprise Hawker Centre operators has already been addressed. The specific details of this prior oral reply were delivered during the 24 September 2025 Parliament sitting. No new measures, timelines, or operational constraints were introduced in this exchange.

results.title: Public Education Plans to Improve Path-sharing Etiquette between Cyclists and Pedestrians
results.summary: The Ministry identified public feedback primarily concerning errant cycling behavior in high-traffic estates. In response, the Land Transport Authority is increasing enforcement checks at tracked hotspots while continuing to raise awareness through education campaigns. These initiatives involve partnerships with the community and schools, alongside direct user education, to improve path-sharing etiquette and adherence to rules governing different paths.

results.title: Rationale for GRIT Programme's Focus on Specific Sectors
results.summary: The Minister for Manpower directed Members to refer to the combined Parliamentary Question reply dated 23 September 2025 regarding the GRIT Programme's rationale and metrics. This previous response addressed the policy focus on financial services, ICT, manufacturing, and wholesale trade sectors, as well as quality measurement beyond traineeship completion. No new details were provided in this session. The specific metrics for placement quality and the full rationale for sector selection remain as previously stated in the 23 September 2025 reply.

results.title: Rationale for Maintaining Distinction between Citizens and PRs' CPF Withdrawal Period
results.summary: The Minister explained that CPF withdrawal rules apply consistently to both Citizens and Permanent Residents, with participation ceasing and full withdrawal permitted upon renouncing status. For Citizens, savings are accessible only after age 55 to safeguard retirement adequacy, while PRs may withdraw full monies upon leaving Singapore. Both groups must set aside the Full Retirement Sum at age 55 to secure a basic lifetime payout from age 65. Unwithdrawn savings for those who renounce status stop earning CPF interest rates. The Ministry balances retirement protection with fairness by applying the same withdrawal framework across all members.

results.title: Rationale for Selection Criteria and Less Generous Compensation under VERS
results.summary: The Minister for National Development confirmed that the Voluntary Early Redevelopment Scheme (VERS) will commence in the first half of the next decade, targeting older HDB estates built around the same period to facilitate orderly redevelopment. VERS will be voluntary and apply only to selected precincts rather than all flats, with compensation lower than the Selective En bloc Redevelopment Scheme (SERS) due to shorter remaining leases. The Ministry aims to firm up policy details, including voting processes and compensation frameworks, before engaging the public, while ensuring non-participating residents remain supported through future upgrading programmes like HIP II. Specific safeguards for seniors facing mortgage shortfalls and the precise voting mechanism remain under consideration and will be shared once the framework is finalised.

results.title: Reason Not Allowing Tax-deductible CPF Top-ups to CPF LIFE Going toward Retirement Sum Scheme Payouts
results.summary: Tax-deductible CPF top-ups for members on CPF LIFE must be streamed into that scheme rather than the Retirement Sum Scheme (RSS), even if the member has met the Full Retirement Sum. This restriction exists because members with at least $60,000 in their Retirement Account are automatically enrolled in CPF LIFE, making RSS payouts irrelevant for them. Consequently, top-ups for CPF LIFE members boost lifelong monthly payouts to prevent outliving savings, while RSS top-ups similarly boost payouts but are limited to a maximum of 20 years or until savings deplete. The Minister clarified that members cannot choose to direct top-ups to the alternative scheme as the two systems operate separately.

results.title: Reasons against Mandatory Rest Periods for Service Workers and Ensuring Compliance with Voluntary Guidelines
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) does not mandate specific rest periods for service workers, citing diverse operational requirements across workplaces. Instead, MOM and the Workplace Safety and Health (WSH) Council issue voluntary guidelines recommending employers allow sitting at intervals and provide anti-fatigue mats or sit-stand stools. These guidelines aim to help employers create healthy work environments tailored to specific circumstances rather than imposing uniform regulations. MOM collaborates with industry associations and unions to promote awareness and adoption of these ergonomic practices while continuing to review occupational health policies based on scientific evidence and international best practices.

results.title: Reasons for 45% of Total Postal Votes Being Rejected for Counting at 2025 General Election
results.summary: At the 2025 General Election, 45% of 6,097 timely-received postal votes were rejected due to faint or missing postmarks, mismatched signatures, or torn envelopes. The Elections Department will strengthen outreach to educate voters on using consistent specimen signatures, posting ballots early, and affixing postage stamps to ensure postmarks. Voters are advised to use express airmail services for greater delivery assurance. Before rejection, envelopes are shown to candidates and agents with reasons explained. The Department will continue these efforts to reduce attrition rates in future elections.

results.title: Recognition of Palestinian State As Diplomatic Tool for Singapore to Move Israel to Resume Talks Towards Two-state Solution
results.summary: The Minister for Foreign Affairs and Senior Minister of State for Foreign Affairs addressed the question regarding Israel's territorial expansion and Singapore's potential recognition of the State of Palestine during Ministerial Statements on the Middle East situation on 22 September 2025. The response was delivered orally at that specific parliamentary sitting rather than through a new written statement. Details on whether formal recognition serves as a diplomatic tool to resume two-state solution talks or aligns with Singapore's national interest were not explicitly elaborated in the provided transcript.

results.title: Redress Mechanisms Available for Clients of Licensed Debt Collection Companies in Dispute Cases
results.summary: The Coordinating Minister for National Security and Minister for Home Affairs clarified that the Police's regulatory oversight of the debt collection industry focuses solely on preventing problematic collection conduct. This regulatory framework does not govern commercial arrangements between licensed debt collection companies and their clients. Consequently, redress mechanisms for clients facing disputes, such as delayed disbursements, fall outside the scope of current Police regulation. The Minister did not specify alternative redress channels for these commercial disputes.

results.title: Regulation on Freelance Confinement Nannies Working in Singapore Without an Employment Agency Contract
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower states that foreign freelance confinement nannies require a valid Work Permit, which employers can obtain directly or via an Employment Agency to avoid additional costs. The Minister explained that because these services are private, decentralised, and highly personalised to diverse home environments, they are not amenable to standardisation or a mandatory licensing framework. Consequently, there is no policy rationale for requiring an employment agency contract or introducing formal training standards and a public register for these practitioners at this time.

results.title: Regulation on Involvement of Migrant Domestic Workers or Illegal Part-time Workers in Home-based F&B Businesses
results.summary: From 2022 to 2024, the Ministry of Manpower investigated 19 cases involving migrant domestic workers or illegal part-time workers in home-based F&B businesses, resulting in seven warnings and two fines. Employers found guilty of illegally deploying migrant domestic workers face penalties including fines up to $10,000 and potential bans from employing such workers. The Ministry issues regular reminders that deploying migrant domestic workers to employers' own businesses, including home-based operations, is illegal. Public complaints and random house visits are used to uncover violations, with remaining cases deemed unsubstantiated.

results.title: Regulatory Oversight over Rail Operators’ Safety Decisions following East-West Line Incident on 25 September 2024
results.summary: Following the 25 September 2024 East-West Line derailment, the Land Transport Authority (LTA) has tightened regulatory oversight by mandating that all maintenance schedule waivers or adjustments be submitted for LTA review prior to implementation.
