Previously, operators like SMRT could extend overhaul intervals based on fleet performance without specific engineering assessments for rare failures, such as axle box issues. The LTA now requires such changes to undergo direct scrutiny as part of licence requirements, ensuring that operator-defined maintenance protocols align with safety standards before execution. This shift addresses concerns regarding the extension of overhaul intervals for the 37-year-old KHI train without prior approval.

results.title: Retention Rates of SG Enable Place-and-Train Trainees After End of Programme
results.summary: Introduced in 2021, the Place-and-Train programme offers up to 90% salary support and course fee subsidies, with 172 employers hiring 467 persons with disabilities to date. From 2021 to 2023, approximately 80% of participants remained employed six months after support ended, while 77% stayed with their original host employers. The programme has been extended for two additional years to place and train another 200 persons with disabilities by 2026. The Ministry of Social and Family Development will continue monitoring outcomes to assess effectiveness in supporting sustained employment and career progression.

results.title: Review in Singapore's Stance in Recognition of Palestinian State
results.summary: Singapore's Foreign Minister addressed recognition of Palestine plans during a Ministerial Statement on the Middle East on 22 September 2025. The Minister did not confirm specific timelines for Singapore to recognize Palestine following reports of France, the UK, Canada, and Australia's intentions for the September 2025 UN General Assembly. No implications for Singapore were explicitly detailed in the statement. Further details on Singapore's stance were not provided in the transcript.

results.title: Review of HDB's Current Homeowner Insurance Coverage
results.summary: The Minister clarified that HDB's mandatory Fire Insurance Scheme covers only building structures and fixtures for mortgaged flats, explicitly excluding home contents and third-party liability to maintain affordable premiums. Flat owners seeking coverage for contents or neighbour impact must purchase additional policies from private insurers of their choice. The scheme applies to all HDB flat owners with HDB mortgage loans for the full loan duration. No review of the current coverage scope was announced in this response.

results.title: Review of Misuse of Drugs Act to Include Stricter Penalties for Cases with Aggravating Factors
results.summary: The Coordinating Minister for National Security clarified that the Central Narcotics Bureau's annual review with the Health Science Authority addresses drug purity thresholds, not net weights, which remain legally encoded. While no trend of syndicates specifically targeting youths for recruitment or sales has been detected, the Ministry of Home Affairs maintains a zero-tolerance approach. Existing Misuse of Drugs Act amendments from 2012 and 2019 already impose enhanced penalties for trafficking to young persons, procuring youth involvement, leaving drugs accessible to children under 16, and permitting consumption by those under 21. Possession of apparatus like vapes intended for drug consumption remains an offence. No new measures regarding aggravating factors or specific apparatus were proposed during this review.

results.title: Review on Anti-Clustering Measures towards Massage Establishments in HDB Town Centres
results.summary: HDB maintains market flexibility in town centre trade mixes but imposes quotas on massage establishments to prevent disamenities. These quotas apply to both HDB rental shops and privately-owned shops seeking approval for a change of use to massage establishments. To preserve residential character, HDB has tightened these quotas and collaborated with the Police to enhance enforcement against errant operators. The Minister noted that beyond quotas, the Police also regulates these establishments, with no specific timelines or new guidelines for siting or renewal currently published.

results.title: Reviewing Quantum of Morning Pre-peak Fares Discount
results.summary: The Public Transport Council (PTC) reviews fare discounts annually, with the 2025 Morning Pre-Peak Fares discount decision expected later this year. Adjustments will consider affordability, financial sustainability, and peak crowding. The current 50-cent discount, introduced in 2017, may change based on these factors. No specific increment has been confirmed yet.

results.title: Risk Assessment and Governance Frameworks for Sustainable Regional Infrastructure and Domestic Investment Incentive Strategy
results.summary: The Green Investments Partnership (GIP) under the Financing Asia's Transition Partnership (FAST-P) initiative has secured US$510 million in committed capital from investors including Export Finance Australia, Temasek, and Allied Climate Partners to support sustainable infrastructure in Southeast and South Asia. Managed by Pentagreen Capital, a Monetary Authority of Singapore-licensed fund manager, the fund employs a governance framework featuring an investment committee and a Limited Partner Advisory Committee (LPAC) to oversee project selection based on investment merits, sustainability impact, and environmental assessments. The Government's initial US$51 million grant serves as catalytic capital to mobilize additional concessional and commercial funding. For future tranches, Pentagreen Capital plans to engage Singapore-based and international institutional investors to reach a target fund size of US$1 billion, with quarterly performance reporting provided to all investors.

results.title: Risk Assessment of Earthquake Occurrences in Areas Close to Singapore
results.summary: Singapore is geologically stable with low earthquake risk, as it is not on major active fault lines. The 3 September 2025 Segamat earthquake was one of three closest documented events, with an epicentre approximately 120 kilometres northwest. The Meteorological Service Singapore will continue tracking regional seismic activity, while the Building and Construction Authority maintains a building safety framework to ensure infrastructure resilience. The Government will collaborate with research institutes like the Earth Observatory of Singapore to deepen understanding of regional seismic occurrences and update risk assessments if necessary.

results.title: Senior Workers Benefitting from Senior Employment Credit Scheme across Sectors from 2021 to 2025
results.summary: The Senior Employment Credit (SEC) scheme, introduced in 2021, has supported over 500,000 unique senior workers by end-2024. Annual sector-specific beneficiary breakdowns are provided in Table 1, covering the period from 2021 to 2025. The Minister for Manpower confirmed these figures during the parliamentary session. Detailed sectoral statistics are available in the referenced table for further analysis of participation trends across industries.

results.title: SkillsFuture Credit Usage during In-Camp Training Low Activity Period for NSmen's Professional Development
results.summary: The Ministry of Defence will not allow NSmen to use SkillsFuture credits for non-military courses during In-Camp Training (ICT) because military training must take precedence to ensure operational readiness. Permitting such activities would dilute training outcomes and compromise the Singapore Armed Forces' capabilities. While the Ministry supports lifelong learning, NSmen are encouraged to pursue professional development outside ICT periods. NSmen receive call-up notifications up to six months in advance, providing sufficient time to arrange work coverage and plan personal or professional pursuits independently of their military duties.

results.title: Reviewing Regulatory Framework for Integrated Shield Plans and Assessing Practice of Pre-authorisation
results.summary: The Government will continue reviewing suggestions to strengthen the regulatory framework for Integrated Shield Plans (ISPs) while clarifying that rising healthcare costs stem from demographics, chronic diseases, and consumption patterns rather than regulation alone. A multi-pronged approach is required to address escalating premiums and insurer practices, such as refusing pre-authorisation certificates or offering "as-charged" coverage for non-cancer treatments. Safeguards will be assessed to ensure policyholders retain full benefits if insurers alter rules unilaterally, alongside potential measures like a dedicated healthcare insurance law and mandatory dispute resolution platforms. The Government will evaluate proposals to prevent insurers from influencing healthcare decisions and will determine whether to publish insurer performance data, including approval rates and turnaround times, to enhance transparency and protect policyholders, particularly seniors, from adverse rule changes.

results.title: Specialised Training in Student Welfare against Bullying for Educators and School Leaders from 2020 to Date
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that the number of teachers and school leaders receiving specialised training in bullying intervention and distress recognition from 2020 to date was addressed in the oral reply to Parliamentary Questions 1 to 12 on 23 September 2025. The transcript does not specify the exact count of trained personnel or confirm whether this training has been made mandatory. Further details regarding the total number of participants and the mandatory status of the training are not provided in this record.

results.title: Statistics and Plans for National School Games Events Held at Singapore Sports Hub
results.summary: From 2022, National School Games (NSG) events resumed at the Singapore Sports Hub, starting with four sports and expanding to 14 sports this year. The Ministry of Education will collaborate with the Ministry of Culture, Community and Youth and SportSG to host a diverse range of NSG events at the Hub over the next few years. This initiative aims to allow future student cohorts to compete at Singapore's premier sporting facility, thereby inspiring them to pursue excellence in their chosen sports.

results.title: Statistics on School Counsellors in Primary and Secondary Schools and Measures to Improve Early Mental Health Intervention for Students
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that the question regarding school counsellor deployment numbers, student-to-counsellor ratios, and measures for early mental health intervention has been addressed via a written answer dated 23 September 2025. Users are directed to refer to the response to Parliamentary Question No 70 in the 23 September 2025 Order Paper for the specific data and details. No further oral elaboration was provided during this session.

results.title: Strategies to Enhance Outreach and Engagement with SME Owners in Heartland and Traditional Business Sectors
results.summary: The Government engages SMEs via Trade Associations, SME Centres, and Enterprise Singapore, which enhanced advisory services in 2024 to assist over 2,000 heartland enterprises. Specific support includes the Enhanced Visual Merchandising Programme, Heartland Enterprise Placemaking Grant, and the Sprout@AMK testbed for the Heartland Innovation and Transformation (HIT) programme. HIT participants receive a 10% rental discount for three years upon transitioning to HDB shop spaces under the Enhanced Entrepreneur Scheme. Additionally, the National Heritage Board offers the SG Heritage Business Scheme and Organisation Transformation Grant for heritage businesses, while an Inter-Agency Task Force co-chaired by Ms Low Yen Ling and Assoc Prof Dr Faishal Ibrahim was established in February 2025 to develop policies for heritage business sustainability.

results.title: Student Intake for Mandarin-medium Postgraduate Programmes in Autonomous Universities
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that the question regarding student intake, rationale, and local student share for Mandarin-medium postgraduate programmes in autonomous universities was addressed via a written answer on 23 September 2025. Users should refer to Parliamentary Question No 96 in the 23 September 2025 Order Paper for the specific data and details. No further oral clarification or updated figures were provided during this session.

results.title: Sufficiency of Public Sports Facilities for Kolam Ayer and Jalan Besar Residents Following Closure of Nearby Sports Centres
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth stated that the closure of Kallang Basin ActiveSG Swimming Complex and St Wilfred ActiveSG Sport Centre will facilitate public housing plans at those sites. To maintain sports capacity for Kolam Ayer and Jalan Besar residents, new Sport-in-Precinct facilities are scheduled for completion in Kolam Ayer and Whampoa by year-end. Residents can continue using existing nearby facilities, including ActiveSG Swimming Complexes at Serangoon, Geylang East, and Jalan Besar; squash and tennis courts at Kallang, Burghley, and Kallang Tennis Centres; and dual-use sports halls and fields at Bendemeer Primary and Secondary Schools. The Government encourages residents to utilize these new and existing options to sustain active lifestyles.

results.title: Time Limit for Prime Minister's Speech
results.summary: Members moved to exempt the Prime Minister's speech from Standing Order No 48(8), which mandates a time limit. This procedural motion seeks to remove the standard duration constraint for the speech. The request requires the Speaker's consent and general assent from Members present to proceed. No specific alternative time limit or new policy measure was proposed in this segment.

results.title: Time Taken between Debt Recovery by Licensed Debt Collection Companies and Disbursement to Clients
results.summary: The Police do not collect or publish statistics on the time taken between debt recovery by licensed debt collection companies and disbursement to clients. This lack of data collection stems from the Police's regulatory focus on preventing problematic debt collection conduct rather than overseeing commercial arrangements between companies and their clients. Consequently, no tracking mechanisms exist to evaluate performance regarding these specific timelines, and the information remains unavailable to the public.

results.title: Timeline for Relaxation of 15-month Wait-out Period Rule for Private Property Downgraders
results.summary: The Minister for National Development stated that the timeline for relaxing the 15-month wait-out period for private property downgraders was addressed in the reply to Parliamentary Question No 112 on the Order Paper dated 22 September 2025. The Ministry did not provide a new update or confirm any interim measures to shorten the wait-out period during this session. Users seeking the specific timeline details should refer to the original parliamentary reply for the complete information.

results.title: Tracking Economic and Social Contributions of Single Family Offices from 2020 to 2024 and Plans to Attract SFOs
results.summary: The Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) tracks Single Family Office (SFO) contributions via assets under management, employment, and wages, while industry bodies like Soristic Impact Collective monitor philanthropic giving separately. To attract SFOs, the Government is revamping the family office tax scheme application process, reducing processing times from 12 months to three months. Concurrent efforts include streamlining private bank account opening procedures to enhance customer experience while maintaining sound controls. These measures aim to strengthen Singapore's position as an international wealth management centre, generate local jobs, and support social and philanthropic sectors without imposing new social contribution tracking requirements on SFOs.

results.title: Unregistered or Non-compliant PMDs Seized since June 2021
results.summary: Since June 2021, the Land Transport Authority (LTA) has seized over 3,500 non-compliant personal mobility devices (PMDs) and power-assisted bicycles (PABs) from public paths, roads, and retailer raids. The agency maintains a firm stance against importing, selling, or using non-compliant devices, including those with non-original batteries, with immediate seizure and enforcement actions taken. While LTA will continue reviewing guidelines on safe storage and charging of active mobility devices within HDB flats, no specific new measures were announced beyond existing enforcement and public education efforts via social media.

results.title: Update on Animals and Birds Act’s Review's Timeline Completion
results.summary: The Minister for National Development directed that the written reply regarding the Animals and Birds Act review timeline, potential penalty increases, enforcement collaboration, and community disamenity reduction be addressed via written replies to Parliamentary Questions Nos 64, 65, 66, and 67. These responses were scheduled for the Order Paper on 22 September 2025. No oral details on the specific timeline, penalty amounts, or collaboration mechanisms were provided during this session.

results.title: Update on Positive Use Guide on Technology and Social Media and Plans for Implementation
results.summary: The Positive Use Guide on Technology and Social Media, developed jointly with the Ministry of Digital Development and Information and the Ministry of Social and Family Development, will be released on the Digital for Life website in the fourth quarter of 2025. The Ministry of Education will introduce relevant portions of the Guide to primary, secondary, and pre-university students through Cyber Wellness lessons. Additionally, pertinent guidance will be shared with parents and families via platforms such as Parents Gateway, Families for Life, and community touchpoints.

results.title: Update on Reviewing Need for Mandatory Training and Certification for Home-based Food Business Owners
results.summary: The Singapore Food Agency (SFA) maintains a risk-based approach, currently not mandating the Workforce Skills Qualification Food Safety Course Level 1 for home-based food business owners due to their low operational scale and associated food safety risks. While these owners are encouraged to complete the course voluntarily, SFA regularly reviews its food safety regime to ensure regulations remain adequate and effective. The Minister confirmed that the review of the need for mandatory training is an ongoing process to manage risks within the evolving landscape.

results.title: Usage of CPF Savings to Fund Children's Overseas Tertiary Education
results.summary: The Minister for Manpower stated there are no plans to allow parents' CPF savings to fund children's overseas tertiary education. The CPF Education Loan Scheme remains confined to basic full-time local degrees at approved institutions due to the scheme's primary objective of securing retirement, healthcare, and housing savings. Overseas education costs are significantly higher and lack the heavy subsidies provided to local institutions, creating a repayment risk that could impact parents' retirement funds. Consequently, students pursuing overseas degrees must rely on alternative financing options such as study loans from financial institutions rather than CPF funds.

results.title: Use of Copyright Law to Strengthen Protection Against Deepfakes
results.summary: Singapore is not adopting Denmark's specific approach of using copyright law to remove deepfakes, as the primary purpose of that law is to support innovation rather than regulate technology misuse. Instead, the Ministry of Law and Ministry of Digital Development and Information will introduce new legislation to empower victims of online harms, including deepfakes, to obtain relief and seek accountability. Beyond copyright, existing intellectual property laws, such as the common law action of passing off, may be utilized for cases involving misrepresentation from digital replicas of identifiable traits without consent. The Government acknowledges the risks of deepfakes and is taking a comprehensive approach to enhance online safety through these combined measures.

results.title: Value of CDC Vouchers Remain Unspent
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth stated that since the digital CDC Vouchers Scheme launched in December 2021 and July 2025, approximately $2.3 billion has been spent. Of this total, $1.26 billion was utilized at participating hawkers and heartland merchants, while $1.04 billion was spent at supermarkets. The scheme maintains separate categories to balance household expense relief with support for local merchants. Regarding the specific query on unspent 2024 tranche values and future flexibility for category mixes, the Minister did not provide specific figures or confirm additional flexibility in the response.

results.title: Value-add and Number of Singaporean Jobs Provided by Companies Given Carbon Tax Relief
results.summary: Companies in the chemicals, electronics, and biomedical manufacturing sectors, which receive carbon tax allowances, contributed over $75 billion in value-add and approximately 160,000 Singaporean jobs. These figures represent nearly 70% of total manufacturing value-added and more than 30% of manufacturing employment. The Deputy Prime Minister and Minister for Trade and Industry provided these statistics using the latest available data.

results.title: President's Speech
results.summary: The President's Speech anchors Singapore's future on a "we first" society where the family serves as a public good essential for workforce strength, national defence, and social cohesion. Addressing the "Singapore Puzzle," the Government pledges to equalise preschool subsidies regardless of maternal employment status and expand paid caregiving leave beyond the current two to six days annually. Policy responses also include reducing mental health stigma through integrated community services, reforming the Certificate of Entitlement system to better reflect equity, and shifting economic focus from GDP to GNP to ensure inclusive growth. The Government commits to studying all feedback on issues ranging from youth anxiety and vaping deterrence to the strategic adoption of intelligent robotics, ensuring no citizen is left behind amidst technological disruption and demographic shifts.

results.title: Voting Thresholds for VERS Exercise Approvals and Resource Management Provided for Community Disputes
results.summary: The Minister for National Development stated that the specific voting threshold for Voluntary Early Redevelopment Scheme (VERS) exercise approvals and the resources HDB will provide to manage community disputes have already been addressed in the oral reply to Parliamentary Questions 5, 6, and 7 on the Order Paper dated 24 September 2025. No new details regarding these measures were provided during this session.

results.title: Weighing Intangible Civic and Heritage Value of Public Library Against Economy Value of Land
results.summary: The National Library Board (NLB) relocates standalone libraries to integrated hubs or malls to maximize accessibility, ensuring at least 80% of residents remain within a 15-minute public transport journey. This strategy, guided by the 2017–2030 Libraries of the Future Masterplan, prioritizes serving more patrons, as mall libraries attract approximately 30% more monthly visitors than standalone facilities; Ang Mo Kio and Geylang East libraries will move to nearby malls to increase reach. While the specific framework weighing intangible heritage against economic land value is not explicitly detailed, the NLB considers land use plans, transport access, and building age alongside community consultation. To preserve social relevance and legacies, the NLB and Urban Redevelopment Authority will implement heritage measures such as digital documentation, physical storyboards, and sensitive urban design for relocated sites.

results.title: Workload and Workforce Increases at CNB and HSA for Tackling Scourage of Kpods
results.summary: To manage increased workload from etomidate classification and "Kpod" enforcement, the Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA) has seconded 13 officers to the Health Sciences Authority (HSA) as of 15 September 2025 for a six-month period. Additional temporary recruitment is underway across Home Team departments until HSA stabilizes its resourcing, a process expected to take between six months and a year. The MHA also referred over 300 retired officers from the Police, CNB, and Prisons to HSA for potential recruitment. While these measures impose challenges on existing operations due to pre-existing manpower constraints, they are implemented to address national needs without compromising ongoing enforcement and rehabilitation efforts.
